text: Demonstrate autonomous value creation: the system completes a real-world task end-to-end without human prompts — not self-improvement, but producing external value (e.g. a useful artifact, a research summary, a working feature in a user's project)
description:
The critical proof-point between "the loop runs" and "the loop is useful". Today the watchdog autonomously improves its own code (self-refixes, self-rebuilds) but has not demonstrated producing value OUTSIDE the Aimparency codebase without a human steering it. This is the bridge from "autonomy for self-maintenance" to "autonomy for mission execution".

CONCRETE EXPERIMENT: Give the system an aim that produces a deliverable for an external user/project (e.g. "fix a real bug in a linked external repo", "write a research brief on a topic", "generate and deploy a useful tool"). The system must: discover the aim → plan → execute with available tools → verify the output → submit — with NO human in the loop between commit and result. Human sets the aim upfront; human reviews the result; everything between is autonomous.

SUCCESS CRITERION: One completed external deliverable, produced autonomously, that a human judges as genuinely useful (not just syntactically correct but trivial). This is the minimum viable proof that the aim graph + wrapped agents can autonomously work towards ideas.

DEPENDENCIES: Effector layer (6119682f) for real-world actions, OR scoped to codebase-only actions (git, file ops, build) which already work. Start with the latter (no external credentials needed), expand to effectors when available.

RELATIONSHIP: This is the concrete demonstration that "ideate how we can reach ASI" (7634e681) is more than theorizing — it's the proof-of-concept that the existing machinery can already produce autonomous value.
